content.problemStatement: Can you make one big triangle using four triangles? You can gum up two figures by common side(for instance, you have 2 triangles (3, 4, 5), (3, 4, 5) you can make triangle (5, 5, 6) if gum them up with side 4, or (5, 5, 8) with side 3). Common side can be not equal.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of four lines, each containing three positive integers representing the sides of a triangle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output 'Yes' if it is possible to combine the four triangles into one big triangle, and 'No' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1. We can obtain (6, 8, 10). Example case 2. We can obtain (182, 303, 472). Example case 4. We can obtain (168, 259, 259).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to check all possible combinations of the four triangles and see if we can combine them into one big triangle. We can use the triangle inequality theorem to check if three sides can form a triangle.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 4!) — we need to check all permutations of the four triangles
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only need a constant amount of space to store the sides of the triangles and the result
